headline: The agent channel that most international students actually use to apply is content-rich but functionally undiscoverable and duplicated across two URLs; the application itself silently forks across three systems, and the eStudent eApplications launch never appears in the public link graph at all.
what_exists: Not the '1 thin page' the brief assumed: a ~27-page /how-to-apply international cluster on study.unimelb (crawled 24 Jun 2026), with fractured authority. AGENT CONTENT: /how-to-apply/authorised-education-agent (hash 36ece15a07d80710, 9,102 chars of real content) is substantive: sectioned nav (About / Who must apply / Find an agent / nomination form / FAQ), a live external agent directory (eap.ascentone.com/unimelb, linked 4x), a downloadable nomination/authorisation PDF (UM_EdAgentNomAuthorisationForm.pdf), fraud guidance ('Only the authorised education agents listed are trained, approved and permitted... genuinely authorised'), scope limits ('cannot influence selection decisions, guarantee admission, or waive entry requirements'), and CoE/offer scope ('assisting you in accepting an offer... including through the provision of the conditional CoE process'). PORTAL-SEAM SHELL: /how-to-apply/your-online-application (hash 70ed708019098089, 264KB) + /your-online-application/application-help ('Application portal help') + /instructions-to-upload-documents + /fees/application-fee + /indicative-turn-around-times + /conditional-confirmation-of-enrolment. DEFINITIONS GATE: /domestic-and-international-student-definitions (hash b0d344d257df33b8) cleanly defines the domestic/international line (citizenship, PR, temporary resident, dual citizenship) that determines the entire downstream visa journey. study.unimelb is the heaviest estate (median 304KB; agent page ~245KB), slowest to load for the offshore/mobile audience that depends on it most.
friction: [COMPLIANCE/IMMIGRATION] The MANDATORY-agent rule is buried and never reaches the people it binds. The agent page states verbatim: 'Pakistan: All graduate degree applicants', 'India: Any international applicant who completed tertiary studies in India', 'China: All applicants who last studied in China can either apply through an authorised education agent or share their academic transcripts using the CSSD'. For these cohorts the agent channel is not optional but the only valid application path; getting it wrong means an invalid application, delayed/withdrawn offer, and a knock-on delay to CoE and the subclass-500 visa.

[DISCOVERABILITY] The agent page is functionally undiscoverable via in-text links: anchor-text.csv contains ZERO contextual anchors reading 'education agent' or 'overseas representative' anywhere in the 20-domain estate. It is reachable essentially only by drilling the /how-to-apply left-nav, so an applicant who does not already know to look will never be told the mandatory channel exists.

[PORTAL SEAM] The actual 'Apply now / log in to your application' launch into eStudent eApplications is JS-injected and absent from the crawlable link graph. A whole-estate scan of study.unimelb links.json surfaces no eStudent/eApplicant/prod.ss launch URL; the only application-system URL exposed is studentit.unimelb.edu.au/news/troubleshooting-application-estudent-portal (a troubleshooting article). The 'Apply now' anchors that DO exist in anchor-text.csv point to unrelated systems (mentoring.unimelb x7, ctrs.app.unimelb x4), not the application portal. The single most decisive click in the funnel is invisible to navigation analysis and unguided in the static estate.

[CATALOGUE BLIND SPOT] Per-course 'How to apply' pages (the dominant entry path, ~128 instances across ~2,944 catalogue pages) do not surface the agent rule. Sampled Doctor of Medicine /how-to-apply (hash 8a09ed3b385e7981) links to /your-online-application but contains NO link to /authorised-education-agent or /find-an-overseas-representative. An India/Pakistan/China applicant browsing by course is routed straight to the portal and never told they must apply through an agent.

[BROKEN ANCHORS] The agent page's own in-page section links are malformed with double-hash hrefs ('##navigation-find-an-agent', '##navigation-agent-form'), so the on-page jump links to 'Find an agent in your country' and the nomination form do not resolve.

[LOAD/EQUITY] At ~245KB the agent page (and the 264KB portal page) is among the heaviest in the estate, served to the audience most likely on constrained offshore/mobile connections at the highest-stakes moment.
fragmentation: DUPLICATE URLs, one page: /how-to-apply/authorised-education-agent (36ece15a07d80710) and /how-to-apply/find-an-overseas-representative (368478b78b79f902) are byte-for-byte identical content (both 9,102 chars cleaned, identical text == True; meta byteLength 245,569 vs 245,575) under the SAME title 'Find an authorised education agent or representative'. This is why prior analysis counted '1 page' twice. Two URLs split link equity, dilute search authority, and create a canonical ambiguity at the journey's most compliance-sensitive page.

THREE-WAY APPLICATION FORK (corroborated by prospective-experience-profiles.json): the apply action splits silently across VTAC (UG domestic), the walled eStudent eApplications portal (international + graduate), and a CRM enquiry form. study.unimelb explains the fork only via prose cross-links (e.g. /your-online-application -> 'apply via VTAC' for UG domestic), with no single decision page that says 'which system applies to YOU'.

PORTAL HELP SCATTER: post-submit guidance is spread across at least four sibling pages (your-online-application, application-help, instructions-to-upload-documents, indicative-turn-around-times) plus an off-estate studentit troubleshooting article on a different subdomain (studentit.unimelb.edu.au), rather than consolidated at the point of the portal handoff.

TWO online-application URL variants crawled (70ed708019098089 root + 91d325dcd9a96c93 /application-help), and the conditional-CoE step lives as its own thin page (c2633aa692ce1721) outside the agent/portal cluster.
the_seam: The apply -> portal handoff. The public estate carries the applicant up to the threshold (agent directory at eap.ascentone.com/unimelb; document-upload, fee, turnaround, conditional-CoE pages) and then hands off into the walled eStudent eApplications portal via a launch action that is NOT in the crawlable link graph (JS-injected; only a troubleshooting article is statically linked). A parallel external seam runs through the AscentOne agent directory and the CSSD/CHSI transcript service (chsi.com.cn). For the mandatory-channel cohorts (India, Pakistan, China) the seam is doubly fragile: the applicant must FIRST find an agent via a page that nothing in-text links to, THEN that agent drives the eStudent submission and the conditional-CoE acceptance on their behalf, so the University's public guidance goes dark exactly when the highest-immigration-risk transaction begins.
recommendation: 1) CANONICALISE the duplicate immediately: 301 /find-an-overseas-representative to /authorised-education-agent (or set rel=canonical) to consolidate authority on the single compliance-critical page. 2) SURFACE the mandatory-agent rule where applicants actually are: inject an 'If you studied in India/Pakistan/China you must apply through an authorised education agent' callout into every per-course /how-to-apply page and into /your-online-application BEFORE the portal launch, each deep-linking the agent directory. 3) MAKE THE PORTAL SEAM VISIBLE AND OWNED: add an explicit, statically-linked 'Start / log in to your application (eStudent)' CTA and a one-screen 'which application system applies to you' decision page (VTAC vs eStudent vs agent vs enquiry) so the three-way fork is resolved before the handoff, not after. 4) FIX the malformed double-hash in-page anchors on the agent page. 5) ADD a short post-submit roadmap at the seam (offer -> deposit -> conditional then full CoE -> visa -> OSHC) so applicants understand what the portal triggers next. 6) WEIGHT BUDGET: trim the ~245-264KB page weight on these offshore-critical pages.
